preview_text:
The model favors Egypt at 47% to lift the match, with New Zealand positioned as the underdog at 23%. A draw accounts for the remaining 30%, suggesting the teams are moderately separated in expected quality but not dramatically so.

The spread reflects a competitive encounter where draws carry real weight. Egypt holds a meaningful edge in the win column, yet neither side is overwhelmingly favored to claim three points outright. The 24-point gap between Egypt's win probability and New Zealand's indicates a contest tilted toward the African side, though the 30% draw likelihood underscores that a one-goal result or stalemate remains plausible.

The sportsbook consensus aligns broadly with the model, moving Egypt's win probability to 55% and tightening the draw to 26%. The gap between the two sources is under five percentage points across all outcomes, suggesting general agreement on the fundamental shape of the match. Polymarket prices diverge sharply, assigning equal probability to all three outcomes; the dashboard makes no judgment on which source reflects true likelihood.
